questions.question: State and prove the Pigeonhole Principle
questions.answer:
Statement: If n items placed in m containers where n > m, at least one container has more than one item.

Proof by contradiction:
Assume each container has at most 1 item.
Max items = m × 1 = m
But we have n items where n > m.
Contradiction! Hence at least one container has > 1 item.

Generalized: ⌈n/m⌉ items in at least one container.

questions.question: In a group of 100 people, at least how many share the same birth month?
questions.answer:
By Pigeonhole: n=100 people, m=12 months
Minimum = ⌈100/12⌉ = ⌈8.33⌉ = 9
At least 9 people share the same birth month.
